briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the interrupt example using the DsiTxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will set up the system with interrupts handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the MIPI DSI Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the system setup failed.
XST_SUCCESS should never return since this function, if setup was successful, is blocking.

simplesect.para: If system setup was successful, this function is blocking in order to illustrate interrupt handling taking place for HPD events.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XDsiTxSs_SetCallback() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDsiTxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XDSI_HANDLER_UNSUPPORT_DATATYPE Un support data type detected XDSI_HANDLER_PIXELDATA_UNDERRUN Byte Stream FIFO starves for Pixel during HACT transmission XDSI_HANDLER_OTHERERROR Any other type of interrupt has occurred like Stream Line Buffer Full, Incorrect Lanes, etc XDSI_HANDLER_CMDQ_FIFOFULL Command queue FIFO Full

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallbackFunc

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxSs instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: This function will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxSs instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Packet level error event is received by the MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DsiTxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the DsiTxSs_PixelUnderrunEventHandler driver function to set this function as the handler for Packet level error event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a DPHY level error event is received by the MIPI DSI Rx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DsiTxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the DsiTxSs_UnSupportDataEventHandler driver function to set this function as the handler for Unsupported data error event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Command Queue FIFO full error event is received by the MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DsiTxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the DsiTxSs_CmdQFIFOFullEventHandler driver function to set this function as the handler for Command Queue FIFO Full error event.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XDsiTxSs interrupt example.

detaileddescription.para: If the DsiTxSs_IntrExample function which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for the interrupts. Notify the events

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful.

simplesect.para: Unless setup failed, main will never return since DsiTxSs_IntrExample is blocking (it is waiting on interrupts for Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) events.

briefdescription.para: For Microblaze we use an assembly loop that is roughly the same regardless of optimization level, although caches and memory access time can make the delay vary.

detaileddescription.para: Just keep in mind that after resetting or updating the PHY modes, the PHY typically needs time to recover.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the XDsiTxSs driver with interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This will provide interrupts for pixel under run and undefined data type

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 11/2/16 Initial Release for MIPI DSI TX subsystem 1.1 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. ms 04/05/17 Added tabspace for return statements in functions for proper documentation while generating doxygen. 2.4 ml 11/15/23 Fix compilation errors reported with -std=c2x compiler flag
